name: OpenCLIP (Open CLIP Models)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); LAION / ML Foundations; no API costs

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub token for private models

performance.notes: GPU inference in milliseconds; batch processing efficient; ViT-B/32 ~ 10ms per image

agent_readiness.notes: Open source reimplementation and extension of OpenAI's CLIP. 100+ pretrained models on LAION datasets. Models: ViT-B/32, ViT-L/14, ViT-H/14, ViT-G/14, EVA CLIP, SigLIP, CLIPA. Encode images and text to a shared embedding space. Zero-shot classification, image-text similarity, cross-modal retrieval. create_model_and_transforms() API. Used for: zero-shot image classification, image search, multimodal embeddings, DALL-E style training. Works with FAISS/ChromaDB for image retrieval.

gotchas: GPU strongly recommended for acceptable inference speed
Model zoo is large — choosing right model requires benchmarking
Zero-shot accuracy varies significantly by domain
best_when: Zero-shot image classification without labeled training data
Embedding images for vector search (combined with FAISS/Qdrant)
Building multimodal AI applications without proprietary APIs
avoid_when: Supervised image classification (fine-tuned ViT or CNN better)
CPU-only environments (too slow for real-time use)
Applications needing simple OpenAI CLIP only (use openai/clip directly)
summary: OpenCLIP is the open-source CLIP ecosystem with 100+ pretrained models on LAION datasets. Encodes images and text to a shared embedding space for zero-shot classification, image-text retrieval, and multimodal search. Includes EVA CLIP, SigLIP, CLIPA variants. Works with FAISS/Qdrant for scalable image search. Essential for building multimodal AI applications without proprietary API costs.
